## Essays

## [Labor, Work, Action](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/labor-work-action/)

– _In Amor Mundi_ , pp. 29-42. Springer Netherlands, 1987.

Abstract: For this short hour, I should like to raise an apparently odd question. My question is: What does an active life consist of? What do we do when we are active? In asking this question, I shall assume that the age-old distinction between two ways of… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/labor-work-action/)

## [Collective Responsibility](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/collective-responsibility/)

– _In Amor Mundi_ , pp. 43-50. Springer Netherlands, 1987.

Abstract: Although I agree with what I think are the two main statements of Mr. Feinberg’s paper, I must admit that I had some difficulty with it. My agreement concerns his firm distinction between guilt and responsibility. “Collective responsibility,”… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/collective-responsibility/)

## [Thinking – Part III](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-part-iii/)

– _The New Yorker_ , December 5, 1977.

Abstract: Reflections  about thinking. Writer gives the answer of Greek thinkers to the question: “What makes us think?” They felt that philosophizing transforms mortals into godlike creatures. In pre-philosophic Greece men strove for immortality by… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-part-iii/)

## [Thinking – Part II](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-part-ii/)

– _The New Yorker_ , November 28, 1977.

Abstract: Reflections about thinking. Thinking, willing, and judgment are the three basic mental activities; they cannot be derived from each other and they cannot be reduced to a common denominator. To the question “What makes us think?” there is… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-part-ii/)

## [Thinking – Part I](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-part/)

– _The New Yorker_ , November 21, 1977.

Abstract: Reflections about thinking. Writer gives the reasons why she is preoccupied with mental activities: 1) The thoughtlessness of evil as demonstrated by the Nazi Adolf Eichmann at his trial in Jerusalem; 2) What are we doing when we do nothing but… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-part/)

## [Home to Roost: A Bicentennial Address](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/home-roost-bicentennial-address/)

– _New York Review of Books_ , 26 June 1975, pp. 3-6.

(Reprinted in S.B. Warner, The American Experiment. Boston: Houghton Mifflin Co., 1976, pp. 61-77, with Arendt’s comments.) Introduction: The crises of the Republic, of this form of government and its institutions of liberty, could be detected for… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/home-roost-bicentennial-address/)

## [Remembering Wystan H. Auden, Who Died In the Night Of the Twenty-Eighth Of September, 1973](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/remembering-wystan-h-auden-died-night-twenty-eighth-september-1973/)

– _The New Yorker_ , January 20, 1975.

Summary: Reflections about memories of the poet Wystan Auden, who died Sept. 28, 1973. Quotes from several of his poems; gives comments of some who wrote about him; discusses his personality & work. Writer met Auden in 1958 and they were good friends.

## [Washington’s ‘Problem-Solvers’: Where They Went Wrong](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/washingtons-problem-solvers-went-wrong/)

– _New York Times,_ 5 April 1972, Op-Ed page.

## [Lying in Politics: Reflections on The Pentagon Papers](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/lying-politics-reflections-pentagon-papers/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 17/8 (18 November 1971): 30-39.

Reprinted in Crises of the Republic. Introduction: The Pentagon Papers, like so much else in history, tell different stories, teach different lessons to different readers. Some claim they have only now understood that Vietnam was the “logical” outcome of… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/lying-politics-reflections-pentagon-papers/)

## [Martin Heidegger at Eighty](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/martin-heidegger-eighty/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 17/6 (21 October 1971): 50-54. Translated by Albert Hofstadter.

(Originally in German, Merkur 10 [1969]: 893-902. Translated by Albert Hofstadter. Reprinted in English in Michael Murray, ed., Heidegger and Modern Philosophy. New Haven: Yale University Press, 1978.) Introduction: Martin Heidegger’s eightieth birthday… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/martin-heidegger-eighty/)

## [Thinking and Moral Considerations: A Lecture](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thinking-moral-considerations-lecture/)

– _Social Research_ , 38(3) (Autumn 1971): 417–46. Reprinted in _Social Research_ , 51(1) (Spring 1984): 7–37.

Reprinted in Responsibility and Judgment.

## [Thoughts on Politics and Revolution (Interview)](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/thoughts-politics-revolution-interview/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 16/7 (22 April 1971): 8-20.

An interview conducted by Adelbert Reif in the summer of 1970, translated by Denver Lindley; reprinted in Crises of the Republic.

## [Reflections: Civil Disobedience](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/reflections-civil-disobedience/)

– _The New Yorker_ , September 12, 1970.

Abstract: Writer discusses the grave threat to our judicial system. For many years now the law-enforcement agencies have been unable to enforce the statues against drug traffic, mugging, and burglary. Considering that the chances that criminal offenders in… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/reflections-civil-disobedience/)

## [Distinctions: A letter of reply](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/distinctions-letter-reply-review-j-m-cameron/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 13 (1 January 1970): 36.

Letter to the editor by Hannah Arendt, in response to J.M. Cameron’s review of Arendt’s Between Past and Future and Men in Dark Times.

## [The Technocratic Mind: A letter of reply](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/technocratic-mind-letter-reply/)

– _New York Review of Books_ (19 June 1969).

Hannah Arendt replies to review of her “Reflections on Violence.”

## [Reflections on Violence](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/reflections-violence/)

– _Journal of International Affairs_ , Winter, 1969, pp. 1-35.

Reprinted in New York Review of Books 12/4 (27 February 1969): 19-31. Expanded as On Violence and reprinted in Crises of the Republic. Introduction: Violence, being instrumental by nature, is rational to the extent that it is effective in reaching the… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/reflections-violence/)

## [Walter Benjamin](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/walter-benjamin/)

– _The New Yorker,_ October 19, 1968.

Abstract: Essay on Walter Benjamin, a German-Jewish writer, who died in 1940 & has achieved posthumous fame. Benjamin’s position was that of a free-lance writer but his publications were infrequent & he felt that his father should give him a… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/walter-benjamin/)

## [He’s All Dwight: Dwight Macdonald’s Politics](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/hes-dwight-dwight-macdonalds-politics/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 11/2 (1 August 1968): 31-33.

Introduction: When I was asked to write a brief introduction to the reprint edition of Politics I was tempted to yield to the rather pleasant melancholy of “once upon a time” and to indulge in the nostalgic contemplation that seems to be the appropriate… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/hes-dwight-dwight-macdonalds-politics/)

## [The Conquest of Space and The Stature of Man](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/conquest-space-stature-man/)

– _Between_ _Past and Future: Eight Exercises in Political Thought_ (1968): 265-80.

## [Is America By Nature A Violent Society?; Lawlessness Is Inherent In the Uprooted](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/america-nature-violent-society-lawlessness-inherent-uprooted/)

– _New York Times Magazine_ , 28 April 1968, p. 24.

## [Truth and Politics](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/truth-politics/)

– _The New Yorker,_ February 25, 1967.

Abstract: An essay on the antithesis of truth and politics. While probably no former time tolerated so many diverse opinions on religious and philosophical matters factual truth, if it happens to oppose a given group’s profit or pleasure, is greeted… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/truth-politics/)

## [Bibliography: A letter of reply](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/bibliography-letter-reply/)

– _New York Review of Books_ (1 December 1966).

Hannah Arendt follows up on her review of J.P. Nettl’s Rosa Luxemburg.

## [What Is Permitted to Jove](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/permitted-jove/)

– _The New Yorker_ , November 5, 1966.

Abstract: Profile of Bertolt Brecht, world-famous German playwright & poet. His political biography is a kind of case history of the uncertain relationship bet. poetry & politics. He was a strict adherent to the Communist idealogy all his life. In… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/permitted-jove/)

## [A Heroine of the Revolution (Review)](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/heroine-revolution-review/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 7/5 (6 October 1966): 21-27.

A review of J.P. Nettl, Rosa Luxemburg; included in Men in Dark Times.

## [The Jewish Establishment: A letter of reply](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/jewish-establishment-letter-reply/)

– _New York Review of Books_ (17 March 1966).

Hannah Arendt replies to responses of her essay, “The Formidable Dr. Robinson.”

## [The Formidable Dr. Robinson: A Reply to the Jewish Establishment](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/formidable-dr-robinson-reply-jewish-establishment/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 5/12 (20 January 1966): 26-30.

Hannah Arendt replies to criticism of her reporting on the Eichmann trial.

## [John XXIII: A letter of reply](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/john-xxiii-letter-reply/)

– _The New York Review of Books (16 September 1965)._

Hannah Arendt’s reply to a letter regarding her essay, “The Christian Pope.”

## [Some Questions of Moral Philosophy](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/questions-moral-philosophy/)

– _Social Research_ (1994): 739-764.

## [The Christian Pope (Review)](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/christian-pope-review/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 4/10 (17 June 1965): 5-7.

A review of Pope John XXIII, Journal of a Soul. Translated by D. White; included in Men in Dark Times.

## [Nathalie Sarraute](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/nathalie-sarraute/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 2/2 (5 March 1964): 5-6.

A review of Nathalie Sarraute, The Golden Fruits. Translated by Maria Jolas. Excerpt: When Nathalie Sarraute published her first novel, Portrait of a Man Unknown, in 1948, Sartre, in an Introduction, placed her with such authors of “entirely negative… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/nathalie-sarraute/)

## [The Fate of the Union: Kennedy and After](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/fate-union-kennedy/)

– _New York Review of Books_ 1/9 (26 December 1963): 10.

Introduction: Was this “the loudest shot since Sarajevo”—as a BBC commentator, stunned by impact of the news, said? Does this shot mean that the brief “moment of comparative calm” and “rising hope,” of which the dead President spoke only two… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/fate-union-kennedy/)

## [Man’s Conquest of Space](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/mans-conquest-space/)

– _American Scholar_ 32 (Autumn 1963): 527-40.

## [Eichmann in Jerusalem (The New Yorker)](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/eichmann-jerusalem-parts-v-new-yorker/)

– _The New Yorker,_ in five issues: February 16, February 23, March 2, March 9, March 16; 1963.

Summary: Before it was published as a book, Arendt’s report from the trial of Eichmann appeared in five installments in The New Yorker. Part I Part II Part III Part IV Part V

## [The Cold War and the West](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/cold-war-west/)

– "The Cold War and the West." _Partisan Review_ 29/1 (Winter 1962): 10-20.

## [Freedom and Politics](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/freedom-politics/)

– In _Freedom and Serfdom_ , pp. 191-217. Springer Netherlands, 1961.

Abstract: To deal with the relationship between freedom and political government in the space of a single, short treatise is not possible. Indeed, a whole book would hardly suffice to deal adequately with the subject. For freedom, which is only very seldom… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/freedom-politics/)

## [Tradition and the Modern Age](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/tradition-modern-age/)

– "Tradition and the modern age." _Approaches to Political Thought_ (2009): 73.

## [Revolution and Public Happiness](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/revolution-public-happiness/)

– _Commentary,_ November 1960.

Excerpt: The purpose of the following reflections is to rehabilitate the word “revolution.” No other word, except perhaps “freedom,” will be more urgently needed in the years to come, and no other word, without exception, has been more gravely… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/revolution-public-happiness/)

– _Daedalus_ 89, no. 2 (1960): 278-287.

## [Reflections on Little Rock](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/reflections-little-rock/)

– _Dissent_ 6, no. 1 (1959): 45-56.

## [A Reply to Critics](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/reply-critics/)

– _Dissent_ , March 1959, pp. 179-181.

## [The Modern Concept of History](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/modern-concept-history/)

– _Review of Politics_ 20/4 (October 1958): 570-90.

Reprinted in Between Past and Future. Introduction: Herodotus, who has been rightly called the Father of Western history, tells us in the first sentence of the Persian Wars that the purpose of his enterprise is to preserve that which owes its existence to men… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/modern-concept-history/)

## [Totalitarian Imperialism: Reflections on the Hungarian revolution](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/totalitarian-imperialism-reflections-hungarian-revolution/)

– _The Journal of Politics_ 20, no. 01 (1958): 5-43.

Introduction: As I write this, one year has passed since the flames of the Hungarian revolution illuminated the immense landscape of post-war totalitarianism for twelve long days. This was a true event whose stature will not depend upon victory or defeat; its… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/totalitarian-imperialism-reflections-hungarian-revolution/)

## [History and Immortality](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/history-immortality/)

– _Partisan Review_ 24/1 (Winter 1957): 11-53.

## [Authority in the Twentieth Century](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/authority-twentieth-century/)

– _The Review of Politics_ 18, no. 04 (1956): 403-417.

Abstract: The rise of fascist, communist and totalitarian movements and the development of the two totalitarian regimes, Stalin’s after 1929 and Hitler’s after 1938, took place against a background of a more or less general, more or less dramatic… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/authority-twentieth-century/)

## [The Personality of Waldemar Gurian](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/personality-waldemar-gurian/)

– _Review of Politics_ 17/1 (January 1955): 33-42.

Reprinted in Men in Dark Times. Abstract: He was a man of many friends and a friend to all of them, men and women, priests and laymen, people in many countries and from practically all walks of life. Friendship was what made him at home in this world and he… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/personality-waldemar-gurian/)

– _Commonweal_ , September 24, 1954, pp. 607-609

## [Europe and the Atom Bomb](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/europe-atom-bomb/)

– _Commonweal_ 60/24 (17 September 1954): 578-80.

## [Dream and Nightmare](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/dream-nightmare/)

– _Commonweal_ , September 10, 1954, pp. 551-554.

Excerpt: WHAT image does Europe have of America? Whatever it may be, it is a reflection of actual conditions in this country, it contains an evaluation of America’s role in international politics, and it expresses the attitude of the nation concerned… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/dream-nightmare/)

– _Social Research: An International Quarterly_ 74, no. 3 (2007): 713-726. Written in 1953.

Abstract: The Hannah Arendt Bluecher Literary Trust has granted permission to Social Research to publish for the first time a lecture given by Arendt in 1953, the provenance of which is her so-called Marx manuscripts. The lecture here entitled “The… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/great-tradition/)

## [Understanding and Politics](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/understanding-politics/)

– _Partisan Review_ , vol. 20, no. 4 (July–August 1953): 377–92.

Reprinted in Essays in Understanding: 1930–1954.

– _Review of Politics_ 15/3 (July 1953): 303-27.

Included in the 1958 edition of The Origins of Totalitarianism. A German version appeared in Offener Horizont: Fetschrift für Karl Jaspers. Munich: Piper, 1953. Introduction: The following considerations have grown out of a study of the origins, the… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/ideology-terror-novel-form-government/)

– _Review of Politics_ 15 (January 1953): 76-85.

– _Commentary,_ March 1952.

Excerpt: Léon Poliakov’s excellent book on the Third Reich and the Jews is the first to describe the last phases of the Nazi regime on the basis, strictly, of primary source material. This consists chiefly of documents presented at the Nuremberg Trials and… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/review-breviaire-de-la-haine-le-iiie-reich-et-les-juifs-leon-poliakov/)

– _Commentary_ 11 (February 1951): 201-03.

A review of Robert F. Byrnes, Anti-Semitism in Modern France. Excerpt: Anti-Semitism is a deplorably neglected area of modern history, and every contribution that does more than simply add another title to the formidable library of apologetics,… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/road-dreyfus-affair/)

Used in The Origins of Totalitarianism, Part 2. Abstract: Of the two main political devices of imperialist rule, race was discovered in South Africa and bureaucracy in Algeria, Egypt and India; the former was originally the hardly conscious reaction to tribes… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/imperialist-character/)

Abstract: Peace in the Near East is essential to the State of Israel, to the Arab people and to the Western world. Peace, as distinguished from an armistice, cannot be imposed from the outside, it can only be the result of negotiations, of mutual compromise… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/peace-armistice-near-east/)

– _Commentary_ 1 (May 1946): 1-8.

Introduction: Rereading Herzl’s The Jewish State today is a peculiar experience. One becomes aware that those things in it that Herzl’s own contemporaries would have called utopian now actually determine the ideology and policies of the Zionist… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/jewish-state-fifty-years/)

– _Commentary_ 1 (February 1946): 27-35.

Introduction: Imperialism, which first entered the scene toward the end of the last century, has today become the dominant political phenomenon. A war fought on an apocalyptic scale has revealed the suicidal tendencies inherent in every consistently… [More](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/imperialism-road-suicide/)

## [Proof Positive](https://contemporarythinkers.org/hannah-arendt/essay/proof-positive/)
